methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian is a public-stage transportation company currently valued at $20.7B, having raised $13.5B in total capital. The comparable universe for valuation purposes is constrained: zero public peers meet meaningful filtering criteria, and only five private companies in the transportation sector show sufficient similarity—The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. A narrow peer set requires a minimum of three datapoints filtered by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier; while four same-sector transportation peers exist, none satisfy the filtering thresholds simultaneously. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given Rivian's specialized subsector positioning. Without sufficient narrow comparables, traditional peer multiples cannot be reliably anchored.

This valuation presents acute methodological constraints. The peer universe is too sparse and heterogeneous to support triangulation via multiples or median-based approaches. Alternative methods—such as precedent M&A transaction analysis within frontier transportation, DCF sensitivity around unit economics and production ramp assumptions, or stage-adjusted venture capital method back-solve—would be necessary to establish a defensible valuation range. The current $20.7B public market price reflects equity market sentiment, but absent comparable-company support or detailed operational data, external validation of that mark is not possible within the bounds of this dataset.
